explanation: The phrase "when it is turned on" indicates a trigger event, which maps to the atomic proposition "initStart" (system/controller initialization start). The requirement that "A set of default values for all parameters shall be provided and loaded" indicates an event that must eventually occur, which maps to "defaultParamsLoaded". The phrase "Start-up Mode" at the beginning specifies the context or state during which this loading happens. Combining these, the system, when turned on, enters "StartUpMode" and must remain in that mode until the default parameters are loaded. This is best represented by the "Until" (U) operator. The overall statement implies a global property that this behavior must always hold. Thus, the LTL formula is G(initStart -> (StartUpMode U defaultParamsLoaded)).
